item.title: You can now transfer your chats and personal information from other chatbots directly into Gemini
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Google is launching "switching tools" that, just as it sounds, will make it easier for users of other chatbots to switch to Gemini.

item.title: Wikipedia cracks down on the use of AI in article writing
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The site, whose policies are subject to change, has struggled with the issue of AI-generated writing.

item.title: Mastodon is making its decentralized social network easier to use with its latest revamp
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Mastodon is redesigning user profiles to simplify the experience and better appeal to mainstream users and organizations.

item.title: Netflix confirms it’s raising prices again
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The standard plan without ads now costs $19.99 per month, a $2 increase from the previous $17.99 subscription fee, while the premium plan is also going up by $2 and will now cost $26.99 per month.

item.title: 16 of the most interesting startups from YC W26 Demo Day
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: From redirecting doomscrolling to training humanoid robots, these 16 startups stood out as the most interesting ones from an overflowing YC cohort.

item.title: Google Translate’s real-time headphone translations feature expands to iOS and more countries
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The real-time headphone translations experience keeps each speaker’s tone, emphasis, and cadence intact, so it’s easier to follow the conversation and tell who’s saying what.

item.title: OpenAI abandons yet another side quest: ChatGPT’s erotic mode
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: It's only the latest of several side projects that the AI startup has ditched over the past week.

item.title: Data centers get ready — the Senate wants to see your power bills
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Senators Josh Hawley and Elizabeth Warren want the Energy Information Administration to gather more details about how data centers use power — and how that affects the grid.

item.title: Defense startup Shield AI lands $12.7B valuation, up 140%, after US Air Force deal
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: In one year, Shield AI's value has leaped 140%. This after it won a contract to be the software provider to Anduril's Fury fighter jet for the U.S. Air Force.

item.title: Apple made strides with iOS 26 security, but leaked hacking tools still leave millions exposed to spyware attacks
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Leaked hacking tools threaten the security of millions of older iPhones. Cybersecurity experts weigh in.

item.title: Google is launching Search Live globally
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Search Live allows users to point their phone camera at objects to get real-time assistance, enabling back-and-forth conversations that draw on the visual context from the camera feed.

item.title: Silicon Valley’s two biggest dramas have intersected: LiteLLM and Delve
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: LiteLLM offers an AI open source project used by millions that was infected by credential harvesting malware.

item.title: ByteDance’s new AI video generation model, Dreamina Seedance 2.0, comes to CapCut
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The new model in CapCut will have built-in protections for making video from real faces or unauthorized intellectual property.

item.title: Why hiring the weirdos works
item.dc:creator: Maggie Nye, Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: When you’re building at breakneck speed, hiring a trusted team is crucial for an early-stage startup. Bland CEO and co-founder Isaiah Granet has tactical advice on how the company managed to find hidden talent in unlikely places.

item.title: A major hacking tool has leaked online, putting millions of iPhones at risk. Here’s what you need to know.
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Here’s what we know, and what you need to know, about Coruna and DarkSword, two advanced iPhone hacking tools discovered by security researchers. DarkSword has now leaked online.

item.title: Conntour raises $7M from General Catalyst, YC to build an AI search engine for security video systems
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Conntour uses AI models to let security teams query camera feeds using natural language to find any object, person, or situation.

item.title: Cohere launches an open source voice model specifically for transcription
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Relatively light at just 2 billion parameters, the model is meant for use with consumer-grade GPUs for those who want to self-host it. It currently supports 14 languages.

item.title: WhatsApp can now draft AI-generated responses based on your conversations
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The Meta-owned messaging platform is also launching a way to free up space, the ability to touch up photos with Meta AI, and more.

item.title: A ‘pound of flesh’ from data centers: one senator’s answer to AI job losses
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Fears of AI-driven job loss are growing fast, and they’re fueling backlash against data centers. Sen. Mark Warner suggests taxing them to help workers survive the transition.

item.title: Mistral releases a new open source model for speech generation
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The model, which lets enterprises build voice agents for sales and customer engagement, puts Mistral in direct competition with the likes of ElevenLabs, Deepgram, and OpenAI.
